company.what_they_do: RedCentric (redcentric.site) presents itself as a recruitment and staffing agency, describing itself as offering 'Professional Recruitment & Staffing Solutions Worldwide' with a focus on 'Strategic hiring for long-term business growth.' The site appears to be a small, possibly early-stage or thin-content website. It is a DISTINCT entity from the well-known UK managed IT services company 'Redcentric PLC' (redcentricplc.com), which is a publicly listed UK managed service provider specialising in cloud, network, cybersecurity, and digital infrastructure services — a completely different business.
raw_notes: Homepage tagline: 'Professional Recruitment & Staffing Solutions Worldwide · Strategic hiring for long-term business growth'
The site content is very sparse — no detailed 'about' page, team, case studies, or named individuals found via search.
The email handle 'admamjames' is unusual/appears to be a typo or concatenation of 'adam' and 'james' — may be a name-based email (Adam James? or a typo of 'adamjames').

NOT the same as Redcentric PLC (redcentricplc.com), which is a UK-listed, Harrogate-headquartered IT managed services provider founded in 1997, operating 8 UK data centres, serving public and private sector, listed on London Stock Exchange (LON:RCN).
